title: Copy resolv.conf into container
body:
For DNS to work properly, we need to copy the host's /etc/resolv.conf
into the container during Init().  We do this by copying it into the
containers rundir and then bind mounting it into the container.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I agree we need this for compatibility but long term I think we should default to delegating DNS to the host more directly by having the container talk via a private socket or whatever.  Would require something like annotating images as supporting the protocol.

(There are a massive number of bugs around docker's `resolv.conf` handling such as not tracking host changes, etc.)
@cgwalters I would love to see something like that.  Is there a socket we could leak into the container that  the resolver would use now?
LGTM. Should be good to merge once CI is working again
